+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "grpc-29044-fork-support-is-only-compatible-with-the-epoll1-and-poll-polling-stra",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "grpc: Fork support is only compatible with the epoll1 and poll polling strategies",
+    "description": "Fork support is only compatible with the epoll1 and poll polling strategies. This issue affects 12+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your grpc setup:\ngRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)\nCheck your build output and test logs for errors related to this issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review grpc configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant grpc configuration:\n### What version of gRPC and what language are you using?\n\nv1.44.0\nPython\n\n### What operating system (Linux, Windows,...) and version?\n\nOS X\n\n### What runtime / compiler are you using (e.g."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Fork support is only compatible with the epoll1 and poll…",
+        "description": "I can't reproduce this on my mac with the latest OS X, and grpcio v1.44.0. On Mac, gRPC defaults to use the `poll` polling engine. I'd be curious if your code worked if you ran the same process with the environment variables `GRPC_POLL_STRATEGY=poll`, which should be redundant on Mac, and\n```yaml\nHere are the logs from the script above:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Fork support is only compatible with the epoll1… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I can't reproduce this on my mac with the latest OS X, and grpcio v1.44.0. On Mac, gRPC defaults to use the `poll` polling engine. I'd be curious if your code worked if you ran the same process with the environment variables `GRPC_POLL_STRATEGY=poll`, which should be redundant on Mac, and `GRPC_VERBOSITY=debug`. The first few lines of output will show you which polling engine you're using.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Here are the logs from the script above:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
